headline: How investigators track the causes of wildfires: 'A snail shell and a stone are valuable clues'
description: Firefighters, police and forest agents are searching for the ignition point of the major fire in southwestern France, much as they would at a crime scene.

France sanctions Russian elite cyberespionage unit 'Turla'
description: France accused Russia's Federal Security Service (FSB) of a series of cyberattacks targeting French ministries and a research institute working on sensitive subjects. The Russian ambassador to France will be summoned in the coming days.

headline: 'It would have been enough to learn the terrible lessons from Gaza to anticipate the current impasse over Iran'
description: The deals that US President Donald Trump so readily boasts about seemed destined to get bogged down in the Middle East right from the start.

headline: 'I would do a line before service, hidden in the bathroom': How cocaine is flooding the restaurant industry
description: Cocaine at work (2/3). From fast-food joints to Michelin-starred restaurants, drug use has become as commonplace as it is alarming in an industry driven by the pursuit of performance, a toxic party culture and grueling working conditions.

headline: 'Thank you, dear Hungary, for proving that anything is always possible'
description: Jean-Baptiste Chastand, Le Monde's Central Europe correspondent, writes a letter to this 'hostile and surprising' country he has covered for more than 10 years.

'We were meant to take as many jewels as we could': The confessions of two Louvre burglars
description: After months of silence, two men suspected of stealing the royal jewels described their involvement in detail during new interrogations in June. According to their statements, they were hired to carry out the heist for '€15,000 to €25,000.'

headline: 'If they catch me, it's over for me': Senegal's LGBTQ+ community lives in daily fear of repression
description: In March, Senegal passed a law increasing penalties for same-sex relations, leaving LGBTQ+ people living in fear. Several described their deeply uncertain daily lives, gripped by the constant anxiety of being arrested.

headline: Simon Abkarian, star of 'De Gaulle': 'At school in Lebanon, my nickname was de Gaulle'
description: The actor and theater director plays Charles de Gaulle in the recently released two-part film. In this article, he explains how he prepared for the role of France's World War II hero.
